definition: Categorical sentiment label assigned to a 256-token chunk of a Federal Reserve Beige Book report, taking values in {negative, mixed, positive}. Derived from human annotations on a -1 to 1 scale, with [-1, -0.2) = negative, [-0.2, 0.2] = mixed, (0.2, 1] = positive.
coding_rule: Human raters assign continuous score in [-1, 1] in 0.1 increments to a 256-token Beige Book chunk; bin to {negative if <-0.2, mixed if [-0.2, 0.2], positive if >0.2}.
formal_definition: S ∈ {negative, mixed, positive} where S = negative if score < -0.2, mixed if -0.2 <= score <= 0.2, positive if score > 0.2.
operational_definition: Human raters scored each chunk on a -1 to 1 scale in 0.1 increments (matching VADER scale); scores were then binned into three classes. Test set distribution: 39% positive, 41.5% mixed, 19.5% negative.

definition: Proportion of test-set observations whose predicted sentiment label matches the human-labelled ground truth.
coding_rule: Compute (correct_predictions / total_predictions) on the 200-chunk held-out test set across all three sentiment classes simultaneously.
formal_definition: accuracy = (TP + TN) / N, computed across all three classes simultaneously.
operational_definition: Computed on 200-chunk held-out test set against human gold-standard labels.

definition: Macro-averaged F1 score across the three sentiment classes, weighting each class equally regardless of base-rate prevalence.
coding_rule: Compute per-class F1 = 2*P*R/(P+R), then take unweighted mean across {negative, mixed, positive}.
formal_definition: F1_macro = (1/K) * sum_k F1_k, where F1_k = 2*precision_k*recall_k/(precision_k+recall_k) for class k in {negative, mixed, positive}.
operational_definition: Computed via standard scikit-learn macro averaging on 200-chunk test set.
measures: Per-model macro F1 on 200-chunk test set

definition: Correlation-style classification metric incorporating all four cells of the confusion matrix; robust to class imbalance.
coding_rule: Compute multi-class generalization of the Matthews correlation coefficient over the 3x3 confusion matrix.
formal_definition: MCC ranges from -1 (total disagreement) through 0 (random) to 1 (perfect agreement). Multi-class generalization used here.
operational_definition: Computed on 200-chunk test set against human gold-standard labels.

definition: Wall-clock time required for a model to classify the 200-chunk test set, on a Windows 11 laptop with 32 GB RAM and 3.3 GHz CPU (no GPU), or remote server in the case of GPT-4o.
operational_definition: Minutes elapsed for inference over 200 chunks; pre-training and fine-tuning time are reported separately for BeigeSage.

definition: Categorical label distinguishing the architectural family of a sentiment classifier: lexical/dictionary-based, pre-trained transformer LLM, or fine-tuned BERT-family transformer.
operational_definition: {lexical, pre_trained_llm, fine_tuned_llm}. VADER = lexical; GPT-4o, Llama 3.1, Phi3, Gemma2 = pre_trained_llm; BeigeSage = fine_tuned_llm.

definition: Binary indicator of whether a model received additional pre-training and supervised fine-tuning on domain-specific (Beige Book) text and labels, beyond general pre-training.
coding_rule: 1 if model received MLM continued pre-training on the 1970-2023 Beige Book corpus (~6.8M words) AND supervised fine-tuning on 800 labelled three-class chunks (3 epochs, batch=4); 0 otherwise.
operational_definition: 1 = further-pretrained on 1970-2023 Beige Book corpus (6.8M words) AND fine-tuned on 800 labelled Beige Book chunks (3 epochs, batch size 4). 0 = only general pre-training (zero-shot prompting).

definition: Proportion of test-set chunks a model predicts as mixed minus the human-labelled base rate of mixed (41.5%). Positive values indicate overprediction of the mixed class.
measures: Predicted mixed share minus human base rate

definition: Monthly estimate of the real GDP growth rate from the Brave-Butters-Kelley index, used because official GDP is published only quarterly.
operational_definition: Brave-Butters-Kelley monthly real GDP growth-rate estimate (Brave & Cole 2019).
measures: Brave-Butters-Kelley monthly real GDP growth rate

definition: Standard U.S. headline unemployment rate (BLS U-3) over the Beige Book sample period, used as a macroeconomic outcome variable.
operational_definition: BLS unemployment rate aligned to Beige Book release dates.

definition: U.S. Consumer Price Index measuring aggregate price-level changes; used as an inflation outcome variable.
operational_definition: BLS CPI aligned to Beige Book release dates.
